title: Make every pipeline run idempotent
description: Design writes so rerunning a task for a given partition produces the same result, never duplicates, regardless of how many times it runs.
body: Use when writing any task that loads or transforms data. Do not use blind append/insert into a target — a retry after partial failure then double-counts rows. Actions: scope each run to a partition (e.g. a date) and replace-by-partition or upsert on a key, so rerunning overwrites rather than appends; make the operation a pure function of its inputs. Validation signals include identical output and row counts after running a task twice and reconciled totals against the source. Risk boundary: non-idempotent loads corrupt aggregates silently; if a target cannot be safely overwritten, escalate before enabling retries.
